Title: An important role of L-fucose biosynthesis and protein fucosylation genes in Arabidopsis immunity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first step of de novo GDP-L-fucose synthesis from GDP-D-mannose by 4,6-dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall polymers and glycoproteins in shoot/leaf tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for fucosylation of cell wall polysaccharides and proteins; supports plant immunity including stomatal defense, apoplastic defense, PTI and ETI
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Next-generation sequencing-based mapping of scord6; mutant phenotyping (scord6 and mur1-1); anti-fucose immunoblot; 35S:MUR1-4xMyc complementation; pathogen infection assays; stomatal assays; PTI/ETI assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes L-fucose production and protein/cell wall fucosylation; loss of function reduces α1,3-fucosylated N-glycans and compromises stomatal and apoplastic immunity, PTI, and ETI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCORD6 was identified as MUR1/AT3G51160 carrying a 25-bp deletion. 2. MUR1 encodes the first-step enzyme for de novo GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis. 3. scord6 and mur1-1 showed strongly reduced α1,3-fucosylated N-glycans and abnormal stomatal ledges. 4. mur1 defects increased susceptibility to Pst DC3118 and compromised pathogen/SA-induced stomatal closure, PTI and ETI. 5. 35S-driven MUR1 complementation restored fucosylation and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream entry step for GDP-L-fucose production; first committed step reported in paper context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3118 or DC3000(avrRpt2) infection; flg22; elf26; salicylic acid; ABA
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FLS2; BAK1 (indirectly affected through glycosylation status)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SCORD6 is an allele of MUR1. The immune phenotype is specific for pathogen- and SA-induced stomatal closure, while ABA-induced closure remains normal. FLS2 and BAK1 abundance was not altered, but their N-glycan fucosylation status was changed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or engineering intact GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis may improve disease resistance, but caution is needed due to pleiotropic effects on cell wall structure and development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered stomatal morphology and cell wall/cuticular ledge structure; broader growth/developmental pleiotropy likely due to global fucosylation defects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing / Illumina HiSeq 2500 for scord6 mapping; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose from GDP-L-fucose to core N-glycans in glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of fucTa fucTb double mutant in dip-inoculation, infiltration, and stomatal closure assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FucTB, promotes core N-glycan fucosylation required for normal stomatal and apoplastic defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FucTA and FucTB redundantly modify N-glycans. 2. The fucTa fucTb double mutant showed compromised stomatal closure and increased bacterial growth after dip and infiltration inoculation. 3. Single mutants did not show enhanced susceptibility, indicating redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutant not sufficient for phenotype; function inferred in double mutant context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preserving core N-glycan fucosylation may support disease resistance, especially when multiple redundant fucosyltransferases are considered.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose from GDP-L-fucose to core N-glycans in glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of fucTa fucTb double mutant in dip-inoculation, infiltration, and stomatal closure assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FucTA, promotes core N-glycan fucosylation required for normal stomatal and apoplastic defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FucTB acts redundantly with FucTA in core N-glycan fucosylation. 2. Loss in the double mutant compromised disease resistance and stomatal defense. 3. Single mutants lacked strong susceptibility phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Redundant with FucTA; phenotype demonstrated in double mutant context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target in glycoengineering for immunity, but redundancy and broad glycoprotein effects must be considered.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lewis A epitope formation on N-glycans
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: N-glycans carrying Lewis A precursor structures; GDP-L-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds α1,4-linked fucose in synthesis of Lewis A glycoepitopes on N-glycans
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable effect on susceptibility to Pst DC3118 under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUT13/FucTC is involved in Lewis A-type N-glycan fucosylation. 2. fut13-2 did not show enhanced susceptibility to Pst DC3118. 3. Therefore Lewis A structure integrity was not required for defense in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a tested core metabolism gene with negative evidence relevant to substrate-specific fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely lower priority target for engineering bacterial resistance in this pathosystem.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose to xyloglucan side chains
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defect in xyloglucan fucosylation did not alter bacterial susceptibility under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mur2-1 affects xyloglucan fucosylation. 2. mur2-1 allowed similar bacterial growth as wild type after Pst DC3118 dip-inoculation. 3. Xyloglucan fucosylation was not required for immunity in this assay.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Negative-result pathway gene used to dissect substrate-specific roles of fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low-priority target for this immunity phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose onto O-glycan chains of arabinogalactan proteins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant disease and stomatal assays with fut4 and fut6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with FUT6 in apoplastic defense; double mutant increases bacterial growth but does not impair stomatal closure
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUT4 participates in O-glycan fucosylation of AGPs. 2. fut4 single mutant showed wild-type susceptibility. 3. fut4 fut6 double mutant had increased bacterial growth after dip and infiltration inoculation but normal stomatal closure, indicating a role in apoplastic but not stomatal defense.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target affecting apoplastic immunity, but likely with moderate effect and redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose onto O-glycan chains of arabinogalactan proteins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant disease and stomatal assays with fut4 and fut6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with FUT4 in apoplastic defense; double mutant increases bacterial growth but does not impair stomatal closure
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUT6 functions in O-glycan fucosylation of AGPs. 2. fut6 single mutant did not show increased susceptibility. 3. fut4 fut6 double mutant displayed compromised apoplastic defense but normal stomatal defense.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Moderate-value target for apoplastic defense studies.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OST-mediated transfer of preassembled oligosaccharide to nascent proteins
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Nascent polypeptide asparagine residue; lipid-linked oligosaccharide donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the preassembled oligosaccharide to asparagine residues of proteins as part of the OST complex
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stt3a-2 mutant disease and stomatal assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal apoplastic and stomatal defense; mutation increases susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STT3A is required for N-glycan transfer to proteins. 2. stt3a-2 showed increased susceptibility in dip and infiltration assays. 3. Stomatal responses to Pst DC3118 were compromised.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports importance of multiple N-glycan processing steps in immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important mechanistic target, though broad essentiality of N-glycosylation may limit direct engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic effects due to general N-glycosylation defects

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OST-mediated transfer of preassembled oligosaccharide to nascent proteins
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Nascent polypeptide asparagine residue; lipid-linked oligosaccharide donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the preassembled oligosaccharide to asparagine residues of proteins as part of the OST complex
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stt3b-1 mutant disease and stomatal assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal apoplastic and stomatal defense; mutation increases susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STT3B is a putative OST subunit. 2. stt3b-1 showed increased susceptibility in dip and infiltration assays. 3. Stomatal defense was compromised.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports broad role of N-glycan processing in immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistic value high; direct deployment may be constrained by pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic effects due to general N-glycosylation defects

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates formation of complex and hybrid N-glycans in the Golgi by adding N-acetylglucosamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cgl1-3 mutant disease and stomatal assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal apoplastic and stomatal defenses; mutation compromises both
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGL1/GnTI initiates complex and hybrid N-glycan formation. 2. cgl1-3 exhibited compromised apoplastic and stomatal defense. 3. This supports a broad requirement for N-glycan maturation in immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in paper as complex glycan1 mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mechanistic target for glycoimmunity studies.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gibberellin signaling context; immunity-related cues not directly established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mono-O-fucosylation activates DELLA repressors, thereby negatively regulating GA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Protein fucosylation / signaling-linked control of immunity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Protein mono-O-fucosylation on DELLA and likely other substrates
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Arabidopsis immunity against bacterial infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong contributor to immunity among tested fucosylation genes; spy mutants showed severe susceptibility comparable to mur1-1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spy-3 and spy-5 mutant pathogen assays and stomatal closure assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal apoplastic and stomatal defense; loss causes severe susceptibility to Pst DC3118
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPY encodes an O-fucosyltransferase previously shown to mono-fucosylate DELLA proteins. 2. spy-3 and spy-5 mutants displayed compromised apoplastic and stomatal defenses. 3. Disease susceptibility in spy mutants was as severe as in mur1-1. 4. The effect is unlikely to be explained solely by GA signaling through DELLAs, suggesting additional SPY substrates/pathways in immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely impacts gibberellin signaling and broader growth regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SPY is enzymatic, the paper frames it through regulation of DELLA repressors and signaling; thus classified here as a regulatory gene. DELLA loss did not mimic spy susceptibility, implying non-DELLA immune targets.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for understanding immunity-signaling glycosylation, but engineering must account for growth/hormone signaling tradeoffs.
